macro.title: Macro
macro.subtitle: Cross-asset correlations and macro regime analysis
macro.btcSpxMeaning: High correlation suggests crypto is trading as a risk asset. Low or negative correlation indicates decoupling.
macro.goldBtcMeaning: Positive correlation implies BTC is acting as a store of value. Negative correlation suggests risk-asset behavior.
macro.vixMeaning: Elevated VIX signals market stress and potential flight-to-safety dynamics affecting crypto.
macro.btcSpxDescription: High correlation (>0.7) means Bitcoin is trading like a risk asset — it falls when equities fall. Low correlation (<0.4) suggests crypto independence. Historically, during Fed tightening phases (2022, 2018) correlation spiked to 0.8+, while in crypto-native bull runs (2021, 2017) it dropped below 0.2.
macro.goldBtcDescription: Negative correlation supports the "digital gold" thesis — BTC as a safe-haven asset. Positive correlation means both are driven by the same macro factor (liquidity, inflation expectations). In 2020–2021 both rose on stimulus; in 2022 both fell on rate hikes.
macro.vixDescription: VIX >25 historically coincides with crypto drawdowns of 20–40% as risk-off spreads across all assets. VIX <18 signals complacency — often precedes volatility expansion. Crypto volatility is typically 3–5x VIX; a VIX spike from 20 to 30 often corresponds to BTC dropping 15–25%.
macro.noPriceData: No price data yet. Macro sync runs every 4 hours.
macro.vixBtcDescription: Negative correlation means Bitcoin acts as a hedge against equity volatility — it may rise when fear spikes. Positive correlation means BTC is a risk asset that falls with VIX expansion. In 2022–2023 VIX spikes correlated with BTC drops; in 2024 the relationship weakened as ETF inflows decoupled BTC from macro fear.
macro.vixBtcStrongNegative: Strong hedge — BTC rises when fear spikes
macro.vixBtcPositive: BTC trades with risk — falls on volatility

fundamentals.title: Fundamental Health
fundamentals.sopr: Spent Output Profit Ratio — ratio of sold price to purchase price for on-chain transactions. >1 indicates profit-taking, <1 indicates loss realization.
fundamentals.m2: Global M2 money supply in trillions USD. Broad measure of world liquidity. Expansion historically supports risk-on assets.
fundamentals.soprCapitulation: Capitulation. Sellers realizing heavy losses — often marks a local market bottom.
fundamentals.soprLoss: Loss zone. Sellers breaking even or slightly underwater. Transitional area.
fundamentals.soprProfit: Profit zone. Sellers taking modest profits. Healthy bull market signal.
fundamentals.soprEuphoria: Extreme profit-taking. Distribution phase with elevated reversal risk.

portfolio.riskInterpretationExcellent: Excellent risk-adjusted performance. Your portfolio delivers strong returns relative to risk taken. Well-suited for long-term wealth building.
portfolio.riskInterpretationGood: Good performance with manageable risk. Consider minor adjustments to reduce volatility or increase diversification.
portfolio.riskInterpretationModerate: Moderate returns with notable risk exposure. Review leverage and concentration. Sharpe ratio suggests room for improvement.
portfolio.riskInterpretationPoor: Poor risk-adjusted returns. High drawdowns or negative Sharpe indicate the current strategy may be unsustainable. Consider de-risking.
portfolio.sharpeDescription: Return per unit of total risk. Higher is better.
portfolio.sortinoDescription: Return per unit of downside risk only. Higher is better.
portfolio.maxDrawdownDescription: Largest peak-to-trough decline. Lower is better.
portfolio.calmarDescription: CAGR divided by max drawdown. Higher is better.
portfolio.cagrDescription: Compound Annual Growth Rate over the period.

heatmapMatrix.title: IMPACT HEATMAP
yieldCurve.interpretations.curveShape.no: Long-term rates are higher than short-term rates. This reflects healthy expectations for inflation and growth. Banks receive a positive rate reversal (positive carry), which stimulates lending.

During the normal curve periods (1975-1978, 1983-1989, 2003-2005) equities showed an average return of 12-15% per annum. Crypto assets (in available history since 2013) also showed positive dynamics in this phase. Defensive assets (gold, short-term bonds) have historically underperformed against equities.
yieldCurve.interpretations.curveShape.fl: The curve is flattened - the market is losing confidence

Spreads between short-term and long-term rates are minimal. Investors are not demanding a significant premium over time - or they are expecting a slowdown in growth, or the Fed keeps short-term rates high.

Every flat curve since 1965 has been preceded by either an inversion or a sharp economic slowdown. In 1994-1995 the curve was flat for 8 months, followed by a soft landing without a recession. In 2006 the curve was flat for 4 months before the inversion. During periods of a flat curve, equities showed increased volatility: the average drawdown was -12%.
yieldCurve.interpretations.curveShape.in: An inverted curve is a harbinger of a slowdown
Short-term rates are higher than long-term rates. The market is discounting a sharp slowdown: Investors are moving into long bonds, suppressing yields, while the Fed keeps short rates high.

According to a study by the San Francisco Federal Reserve Bank (Estrella & Hardouvelis, 1991), the 10Y-2Y reversal has preceded economic slowdowns in 8 of 9 observations since 1955. Average lag: 12-18 months. The only exception is 1966 (soft landing without a decline in GDP). With an inverted curve, the S&P 500 showed a median drawdown of -18% over the next 12 months. Gold in the same period historically outperformed by +8-15% due to safe-haven demand.
yieldCurve.interpretations.curveShape.hu: Intermediate-term rates (5-7Y) are higher than long-term rates (10-30Y), but the curve is not yet completely inverted. A rare and short-lived phase - usually precedes a complete inversion or a sharp Fed reversal.

The humpback curve was observed in 1978 (before the Volcker shock), 1989 (before the S&L crisis) and 2000 (before dot-com). Duration of the humpback phase: on average 2-4 months. In this phase, defensive sectors (utilities, healthcare, consumer staples) historically outperform growth by 5-8%.
yieldCurve.interpretations.10y2ySpread.u: Could not get current spread 10Y-2Y. Check your connection to the FRED API.
In the absence of data, historical comparisons cannot be made. Use alternative sources.
yieldCurve.interpretations.10y2ySpread.s: The spread {{spread}}% indicates a healthy time premium. The market expects stable growth and inflation within the target. Banks are actively lending, margin lending is growing.

With a spread of >1%, equities have historically shown an average return of 14% per annum (1996-1997, 2003-2004, 2013-2014). High-beta assets (crypto, growth stocks) in this phase outperform value by 8-12%. Bonds with duration <2 years historically underperform equities by 6-8%.
yieldCurve.interpretations.10y2ySpread.d: The spread is positive, but no longer aggressive. The market is starting to price in the risk of a slowdown, but there is no panic. A typical late-cycle phase—growth continues, but the rate slows.

With a spread of 0.25-1.0%, increased volatility was observed: the average max drawdown of the S&P 500 was -8% for 6 months. In 2018 the spread was 0.5% before the -20% correction (no recession). Dividend shares have historically outperformed growth by 3-5% in this phase. Crypto assets showed volatility of ±30-40% in a three-month window.
yieldCurve.interpretations.10y2ySpread.w: The spread has tightened to {{spread}}%. Every time 10Y-2Y has approached zero in the last 50 years, in the next 6-18 months, either a recession began or the Fed sharply cut rates.

With a spread of -0.1% to +0.25%, the S&P 500 historically showed an average drawdown of -10% over 6 months. Crypto assets in this phase: median decline -25% (BTC), -35% (ETH). Gold historically outperforms by +5-10%. The VIX averaged from 15 to 25.
yieldCurve.interpretations.10y2ySpread.i: The 10Y-2Y inversion at {{spread}}% is active. An inversion has preceded an economic slowdown in 8 out of 9 observations since 1955, according to the San Francisco Fed. The only exception is 1966 (soft landing without a decline in GDP).

During the inversion, the S&P 500 showed a median drawdown of -18% over 12 months. Maximum drawdowns: 2008 (-57%), 2001 (-49%), 1973 (-48%). Gold: median growth +12% over 12 months. after inversion. Crypto (in history since 2013): BTC showed a decline of -40% on average over 6 months. after inversion. Cash and short-term bonds have historically maintained par value.
yieldCurve.interpretations.10y3mSpread.u: 10Y-3M is a key predictor in the NY Fed Logistic Model. In the absence of data, use alternative indicators.
yieldCurve.interpretations.10y3mSpread.s: 10Y-3M at {{spread}}% is the 'safe zone'. The NY Fed model gives <5% chance of a slowdown. Short-term rates are significantly lower than long-term rates, which stimulates lending and investment.

At 10Y-3M >1.5%, recession was observed <2% of the time over the 60 years of data. The S&P 500 grew at an average annual rate of 15%. Crypto assets: median growth +80% per annum (in available history). High-yield bonds outperform investment-grade by 2-3%.
yieldCurve.interpretations.10y3mSpread.d: The spread is still positive ({{spread}}%), but no longer aggressive. The probability of a recession according to the NY Fed model is below 10%. Late expansion phase.

With a spread of 0-1.5%, both soft landings (1995, 2019) and recessions (2001, 2007) were observed. S&P 500: average return of 8% per annum, but with increased volatility. Crypto: ±50% volatility in a three-month window.
yieldCurve.interpretations.10y3mSpread.c: 10Y-3M approaches zero ({{spread}}%). An earlier and more sensitive indicator than 10Y-2Y. The NY Fed uses this spread to calculate the likelihood of a recession.

With a spread of -0.5% to 0%, the probability of a recession according to the NY Fed model has historically been 15-30%. S&P 500: average drawdown -12% for 6 months. BTC: median decline -30% in a three-month window. Gold: +5-8% for 6 months.
yieldCurve.interpretations.10y3mSpread.i: The 10Y-3M inversion by {{spread}}% is a key input for the NY Fed Logistic Model. This spread has predicted every recession since the 1960s. The only exception is 1998 (short-term inversion, rapid Fed reversal).

With a 10Y-3M inversion, the probability of recession historically was 40-70%. S&P 500: median drawdown -20% for 12 months, maximum -57% (2008). BTC (in history since 2013): average decline -45% over 6 months. after inversion. Gold: +10-18% for 12 months. like safe-haven. Dollar (DXY): +3-5% for 6 months. on flight-to-safety. Short-term bonds: maintaining par value.
yieldCurve.interpretations.recessionProb: No data for calculation. The 10Y-3M spread may not be available.
Use alternative indicators: Conference Board LEI, PMIs, unemployment claims.
The NY Fed model estimates the chance of a recession at {{prob}}% for 12 months. Historically low level. The economy is in an expansion phase.

With a probability of <10%, recession was observed in <5% of cases. S&P 500: average return of 14% per annum. Crypto: median growth +60-100% per annum. Gold: ±5% (sideways). Credit spreads: tight, stable.

The model produces {{prob}}% - above the minimum, but not yet critical. A typical phase of the late cycle, when growth slows down, but there is no decline yet. Examples: 2018 (preceded a -20% correction), 1995 (soft landing).

With a 10-25% probability: the S&P 500 showed an average drawdown of -8% over 6 months. In 50% of cases, a recession did not occur (1995, 1998, 2019). Crypto: volatility ±40% in a three-month window. Defensive sectors outperform growth by 2-4%.
{{prob}}% - serious level. The NY Fed model is considered one of the most accurate: predicted slowdowns in 1970, 1973, 1980, 1981, 1990, 2001, 2007, 2020.

At 25-50%: the S&P 500 showed an average drawdown of -15% over 12 months. Recession occurred in 70% of cases. BTC: median decline -35% over 6 months. Gold: +8-12% for 12 months. DXY: +2-4% on safe-haven demand. Credit spreads (HY-IG): widening by 150-250 bps.
{{prob}}% - critical level. These values were observed before: 2008 (peak ~45% in 2006), 2001 (peak ~50% in 2000), 1990 (peak ~35% in 1989). The market is already discounting the decline.

With probability >50%: recession occurred in 85% of cases. S&P 500: median drawdown -25% for 12 months, maximum -57% (2008). BTC (since 2013): average decline -50% over 6 months. Gold: +12-20% for 12 months. DXY: +4-6%. Treasury bonds (10Y): +8-12% total return. Credit spreads: widening by 300-500 bps.
All asset classes are showing positive dynamics. VIX is low, credit spreads are compressed, investors buy growth and high-beta. The 'greed' phase on the Fear&Greed scale.

Risk-on modes historically last 12-24 months. During this phase, the S&P 500 showed an average return of 18% per annum. NASDAQ outperform SPX by 5-8%. Crypto (BTC): median growth +120% per annum. Gold: underperform by -5% (investors prefer yield). DXY: weakens by 2-3% (carry trades).

Markets are falling, VIX is soaring, investors are selling stocks and crypto, moving into cash and bonds. Credit spreads widen as banks stop lending. This is either a correction (-10-20%) or the beginning of a bear market (-30%+).

Risk-off phase: average duration 6-9 months. S&P 500: median drawdown -18%, maximum -57%. BTC: median decline -60% over 6 months. Gold: +10-15% for 6 months. (safe-haven). DXY: +5-8% (flight to safety + Fed hikes). Treasury bonds (10Y): +6-10% total return. VIX: growth from 15 to 35-45.
TRANSITION mode is the turning point

The market cannot choose a direction. Some sectors are growing, others are falling. The leaders of the last cycle are weakening, new leaders have not yet been identified. The most unstable phase is a trap for those who buy the drawdown too early.

Transition phases last 3-6 months. S&P 500: flat ±5% with high volatility. Sector rotation: energy, staples outperform tech by 8-12%. BTC: volatility ±50% without a clear trend. Gold: +3-5% (first signs of hedge demand). DXY: sidewall ±2%.
Cross-market analysis could not determine the current mode. Possible reasons: conflicting signals, insufficient volatility, technical problems.

When the market is uncertain, historically the best strategy is a neutral position with increased cache. During periods without a clear regime, the S&P 500 showed a minimum return of 2-4% per annum with increased volatility.
yieldCurve.interpretations.topAnalog.wea: The similarity is just {{similarity}}%. The current situation is unique and has no clear historical precedent. A soft landing scenario or a new type of crisis is possible.
Weak analogies (<30%) historically preceded both soft landings (1995, 2019), and sudden shocks (2020 - COVID). Do not rely only on analogies - use complex analysis (PMI, NFP, CPI, earnings).
yieldCurve.interpretations.topAnalog.rec: The current curve and macro context are {{similarity}}% identical to {{analog}}. Then the slowdown has occurred.
Lessons from the period {{analog}}. Signal lag: {{leadTime}} months. With similarity >30%, the probability of recession historically was 70%. SPX outcome: {{sp500Outcome}}%.
yieldCurve.interpretations.topAnalog.noR: The current curve and macro context are {{similarity}}% identical to {{analog}}. Then the slowdown has not occurred.
Lessons from the period {{analog}}. Signal lag: {{leadTime}} months. With similarity >30%, the probability of recession historically was 30%. SPX outcome: {{sp500Outcome}}%.
yieldCurve.interpretations.crossMarket.p: With the current shape of the curve, {{name}} has historically shown an increase of +{{impact3m}}% (3M) and +{{impact6m}}% (6M). This is one of the best signals for this asset in the current regime.
In similar periods {{name}} outperform other assets by 5-10%. Risk level: {{riskLevel}}. Volatility is historically 20-30% lower than in the opposite scenario.
yieldCurve.interpretations.crossMarket.n: Moderate positive signal for {{name}}: +{{impact3m}}% (3M), +{{impact6m}}% (6M). Not the strongest scenario, but the direction is positive.
In similar periods, {{name}} grew by 2-5% with moderate volatility. Risk level: {{riskLevel}}. The signal is ambiguous - sideways movement is possible.
Moderate negative signal for {{name}}: {{impact3m}}% (3M), {{impact6m}}% (6M). Sideways or correction of 5-10% is possible.
In similar periods, {{name}} showed a decrease of 3-7% with increased volatility. Risk level: {{riskLevel}}. Not critical, but negative bias is present.
Significant negative signal for {{name}}: {{impact3m}}% (3M), {{impact6m}}% (6M). With the current yield-curve configuration, {{name}} historically underperform. Risk level: {{riskLevel}}.

In similar periods, {{name}} showed a decline of -10% to -25%. Volatility has historically increased by 40-60%. Recovery period: on average 9-12 months after reaching the bottom. Safe-haven assets (gold, DXY) historically outperform in this phase.
yieldCurve.interpretations.aggregatedFor: Aggregated forecast: soft landing or growth ({{prob}}% recession)
Based on {{analogies}} historical analogies, the probability of a recession is low ({{prob}}%). Confidence: {{confidence}}. Most analogies point to continued growth or a mild slowdown.
In <30% recessions, the S&P 500 has historically grown 8-12% annually. Crypto: median growth +50-80%. Gold: ±5% (sideways). Credit spreads: stable.
The model gives {{prob}}% - the zone of uncertainty. The analogies are contradictory: {{analogiesList}}. The market can go both ways.

With a 30-60% probability, the S&P 500 historically showed a sideways trend of ±5% with high volatility. In 40% of cases, a recession did not occur (1995, 1998). Sector rotation: value outperform growth by 5-8%. Crypto: volatility ±60% without a clear trend.
Aggregate forecast: high probability of slowdown ({{prob}}%)
{{prob}}% - Most historical analogies point to a slowdown. Corporate profits will begin to decline 2-4 quarters after the signal.

With >60% probability, recession occurred in 85% of cases. S&P 500: median drawdown -25% for 12 months, maximum -57%. Crypto: average decline -50% over 6 months. Gold: +12-20% for 12 months. Treasury bonds (10Y): +8-12% total return. DXY: +4-6%. Credit spreads: widening by 300-500 bps.
yieldCurve.interpretations.overallAssess: Low risk: {{shape}} curve, favorable historical conditions.
Increased risk: curve {{shape}}, precedent {{analog}}. Historically, a conservative position has been recommended.
High risk: curve {{shape}}, analogy {{analog}}, risk level {{risk}}. Historically, increased attention to capital protection has been required.
yieldCurve.interpretations.forecast.high: Based on {{matches}} historical analogies: high probability of recession ({{prob}}%). Recession scenarios: {{scenarios}}.
yieldCurve.interpretations.forecast.unce: Based on {{matches}} historical analogies: uncertainty (scenario balance).
yieldCurve.interpretations.forecast.soft: Based on {{matches}} historical analogies: soft landing tendency ({{prob}}%). Analogues without recession: {{softScenarios}}.
yieldCurve.interpretations.crossMarketSi: Normal yield curve ({{shape}}) indicates a healthy economy. Favorable environment for risk assets.
The yield curve is flattening ({{shape}}). Recession probability is {{prob}}%. Early signs of stress - defensive positioning recommended.
Deep yield curve inversion ({{shape}}). Recession probability is {{prob}}%. High stress environment - maximum caution advised.
Yield curve is normalizing ({{shape}}). Recovery phase - selective opportunities emerging.
Yield curve is flat or ambiguous ({{shape}}). Uncertainty requires caution.

landing.faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Mirkaso.
landing.faq.q1.question: What is Mirkaso?
landing.faq.q1.answer: Mirkaso is an advanced analytics platform for crypto traders and investors. We combine on-chain metrics, derivatives data, macro analysis, and AI-powered signals into one unified dashboard.
landing.faq.q2.question: Is Mirkaso free to use?
landing.faq.q2.answer: Yes, we offer a free tier with market dashboard, macro overview, heatmap, and manual portfolio tracking. Pro plan unlocks anomaly scanner, entry levels, AI portfolio analytics, and alerts.
landing.faq.q3.question: Which assets are supported?
landing.faq.q3.answer: We cover major cryptocurrencies including BTC, ETH, SOL, BNB, XRP, DOGE, ADA, LINK, AVAX, and POL — with more assets added regularly.
landing.faq.q4.question: How accurate are the trading signals?
landing.faq.q4.answer: Our signals combine multiple data sources including open interest, funding rates, CVD, exchange flows, and technical indicators. Each signal includes a strength score for confidence assessment.
landing.faq.q5.question: Can I use Mirkaso on mobile?
landing.faq.q5.answer: Absolutely. Mirkaso is fully responsive and works seamlessly on desktop, tablet, and mobile browsers.
landing.cta.description: Join thousands of traders and investors who use Mirkaso to navigate the crypto markets with confidence.
oi.description.longBuildup: OI↑ Price↑ Volume↑ — Smart money buying, crowd shorting.
oi.description.shortBuildup: OI↑ Price↓ Volume↑ — Smart money distributing to the crowd.
oi.description.shortCovering: OI↓ Price↑ — Unwinding of previous downtrend.
oi.description.longDistribution: OI↓ Price↑ — Distribution at end of uptrend.
oi.description.shortCoveringBottom: OI↓ Price↓ — Unwinding at end of downtrend.
oi.description.longDistributionCont: OI↓ Price↓ — Unwinding of previous uptrend.
oi.detailed.longBuildup: Eventually the crowd will close shorts at stops, pushing price higher. Sustainable uptrend, strong market.
oi.detailed.shortBuildup: The crowd is buying, but soon they will be stopped out, pushing price even lower. Sustainable downtrend, strong market.
oi.detailed.shortCovering: Smart money taking profit on previous shorts, crowd covering losses. OI reversal coincides with correction beginning.
oi.detailed.longDistribution: Big players taking profit, closing longs. Crowd closing sells at stops. Weak market.
oi.detailed.shortCoveringBottom: Big players taking profit, closing shorts. Crowd closing buys at stops. Weak market, growth expected.
oi.detailed.longDistributionCont: OI reversal coincides with correction beginning. Smart money taking profit on previous longs, crowd covering losses.
oi.detailed.neutral: OI stagnating. Further price movement uncertain. No analysis.
oi.detailed.accumulationPhase: Big players accumulating while price is flat. Conflict between bulls and bears.
oi.detailed.distributionPhase: Big players distributing while price is flat. Positions flowing from pros to the crowd.
oi.detailed.default: Insufficient data for clear interpretation. Market in uncertainty.
oi.tactic.longBuildup: Don't fight the trend. Wait for pullbacks to EMA20/50 to enter long.
oi.tactic.shortBuildup: Enter short on bounces to resistance. Don't catch a falling knife.
oi.tactic.shortCovering: Continuation of correction or reversal expected. Enter after breakout confirmation.
oi.tactic.longDistribution: Decline expected. Do not enter long on this phase.
oi.tactic.shortCoveringBottom: Wait for reversal signs. Enter after bottom formation.
oi.tactic.longDistributionCont: Continuation of correction or reversal expected. Don't buy the dip.
oi.tactic.neutral: Wait for OI and price movement to form a signal.
oi.tactic.accumulationPhase: Wait for breakout on elevated volume and trade in its direction. If accumulation in trend - trade continuation. Range will become support/resistance zone.
oi.tactic.distributionPhase: Prefer trading trend reversal. Range will become support/resistance after price exits.
oi.tactic.default: Waiting for clear OI + Price + Volume pattern to form.
